perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN, and any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries, made, conceived or acquired by him or her no later than one year after the termination of employment, whether made or conceived solely or jointly with others, whether during regular business hours or otherwise; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN whether or not patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN whether or not patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon. The disclosure of any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries made, conceived or acquired by him or her after the termination of employment, is optional, unless FRANKLIN provides compensation at fair market value for said improvement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, the employee was obligated to disclose any improvements to their inventions within one year after termination. The changed text makes the disclosure of these improvements optional unless FRANKLIN provides compensation, creating uncertainty about the employee's obligations and FRANKLIN's rights regarding post-employment improvements and conflicting with the other obligations set out in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN whether or not patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon. The disclosure of any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries made, conceived or acquired by him or her after the termination of employment, is optional, unless FRANKLIN provides compensation at fair market value for said improvement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original obligation for the employee to disclose improvements made after termination. The original text mandated disclosure within one year, whereas the changed text makes this disclosure optional unless compensation is provided by FRANKLIN. Since the contradicted text is identical to the changed text, the in-text contradiction is that the obligation is now optional, creating a direct conflict with the initial requirement of mandatory disclosure, leading to uncertainty about the employee's obligations and FRANKLIN's rights regarding post-employment improvements.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration. However, the application for and transfer of any foreign patents or registrations will require additional consideration to be negotiated between FRANKLIN and EMPLOYEE.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, the employee was required to assign all applications and improvements to FRANKLIN without further consideration. The changed text now states that foreign patents and registrations will require additional consideration, contradicting the initial 'without further consideration' clause and creating uncertainty and conflicting obligations about the terms of the transfer and assignment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration. However, the application for and transfer of any foreign patents or registrations will require additional consideration to be negotiated between FRANKLIN and EMPLOYEE.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that the employee assigns all applications and improvements without further consideration. The changed text adds a clause specifying that foreign patents/registrations require additional consideration, thus directly contradicting the 'without further consideration' clause in both the changed text and the contradicted text. This creates an in-text inconsistency as outlined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation. Divulging information about FRANKLIN's processes, businesses, or customers is permitted with written consent from FRANKLIN's legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, the employee was prohibited from divulging any confidential information related to FRANKLIN's processes, businesses, and customers. The changed text now allows divulging information about processes, businesses, or customers with written consent, directly contradicting the earlier prohibition. This creates uncertainty regarding what information is truly protected and introduces conflicting obligations and conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation. Divulging information about FRANKLIN's processes, businesses, or customers is permitted with written consent from FRANKLIN's legal counsel.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the employee agreed not to divulge any confidential information relating to FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers. The changed text then permits divulging information about FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, or customers with written consent from FRANKLIN's legal counsel. This allowance directly contradicts the previous statement prohibiting the divulgence of such information, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed text, however, the Changed text contains contradictory terms within itself.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE agrees that this Agreement shall be binding upon his or her heirs, executors, administrators or other legal representative or assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE agrees that this Agreement shall be binding upon his or her heirs, executors, administrators or other legal representative. However, this agreement shall not be assigned by the employee to any outside entity.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, the agreement was binding upon the employee's heirs, executors, administrators *or assigns*. The modified text states that the agreement cannot be assigned to any outside entity, contradicting the original assignability clause. This creates uncertainty about the agreement's scope and enforcement in case of assignment scenarios adding conflicting obligations and restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE agrees that this Agreement shall be binding upon his or her heirs, executors, administrators or other legal representative. However, this agreement shall not be assigned by the employee to any outside entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is binding upon the employee's heirs, executors, administrators *or assigns*. The changed text, which is identical to the contradicted text, states the agreement cannot be assigned to any outside entity, directly contradicting the original assignability clause. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement was initially assignable, but the modified text explicitly prohibits assignment, violating the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
